# docs(runtime): document residual sync-then-async pool-leak constraint (#950)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `a5b5b7992fe1` — docs(runtime): document residual sync-then-async pool-leak constraint (#950)

**Body**:

Security-reviewer audit on PR #952 surfaced that the new outer-loop skip
condition has a corner case: ``AsyncSQLDatabaseNode._shared_pools`` is
process-wide (class-level dict), not partitioned per-loop. If a caller
uses the SAME ``LocalRuntime`` instance synchronously first (creating
pools whose reaper tasks bind to the persistent loop), then re-enters
``__exit__`` from inside an outer async loop, those persistent-loop-owned
pools are not disposed by either path — the outer loop's teardown can't
reach them (different loop) and we skip them here.

Pre-existing relative to this PR (the same gap existed before, masked by
the wait_for warning + cross-loop "Future attached to a different loop"
error). Not in #950's acceptance scope; the canonical fix (track which
pools this runtime created + WARN-log the leak signal + Tier-2 regression
asserting empty ``_shared_pools`` post-exit) exceeds one shard's
invariant budget. Documenting the constraint inline so the next reader
can pick up the follow-up with full context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
